column9: AAA Equivalent

column11: Weakness
column12: Upgrade

column12: Use np.cumsum-based vectorized integral-image computation.

column12: Add plunging-fold support (amplitude varies along strike).

column9: Yellowstone travertine terraces reference (Hell Let Loose / Horizon)

column9: Gaea Slump / Skyrim landslide scars
column10: Slope-weighted origin sampling, concave scar + convex deposit fan with 60% volume conservation heuristic — geologically motivated.

column12: Trace downslope path and distribute deposits along travel line; elongate scar uphill only.

column9: Gaea frost-heave micro-relief
column10: Voronoi cell boundaries as polygon ridges — correct physics intuition for permafrost pattern.

column12: Use KDTree or broadcast to single numpy op; add slope-aligned striping.

column9: Horizon Forbidden West coral reef authoring
column10: Uses _distance_from_mask transform; reef band centered on half-width; clamps to sea_level. Honest early-return when coastline absent.

column12: Gate by tidal_range and distance-from-land to avoid reefs in deep-water-hugging coasts.

column9: Honeycomb weathering decals (Megascans)
column10: Steep-slope weighted sampling, elliptical cavity shape; proper intensity gate.
column11: 50 cavities over full heightmap is sparse — honeycomb weathering is by definition dense; cavity depth flat (no interior texture).
column12: Scale cavity count with steep area; layer micro+macro cavities.

column9: Hydrological seepage analysis (Houdini/Arcgis)
column10: Mid-slope band + elevation-contour boundaries — correct two-factor filter for spring emergence.
column11: Layer boundaries placed by elevation quantization — real geology uses stratigraphic dip, not level contours. Narrow bands (exp/0.001) can miss entirely at some resolutions.
column12: Use actual stratigraphy (from terrain_stratigraphy) rather than quantized elevation.

column9: UE5 PCG World Partition biome graph / Witcher 3 region splitter
column10: Voronoi biome distribution with soft transitions, corruption fBm map with separate seed, building-plot flatten zones with padded radius, per-biome climate params, name resolution via alias dict.
column11: Voronoi is fixed-weight — no biome-count rebalancing based on temperature/moisture gradients; flatten zones are circular only.
column12: Climate-driven Voronoi weighting; rectangular flatten zones for linear building footprints.

column9: Houdini Edge Detection / Gaea Slope mask with clustering
column10: Real slope-map gradient + flood-fill clustering + gradient-derived facing angle. Includes dimensional-units bug fix (TERR-001) with proper height_scale propagation.
column11: Python for-loop flood-fill is O(N) but slow on large grids; Dr. cluster rotation uses only center cell gradient (not PCA over cluster); no confidence/overlap score.
column12: Switch to scipy.ndimage.label for O(HW) label pass and fit oriented bounding box via PCA of cluster cells for rotation.

column9: UE Landscape layer blend / Witcher 3 biome transition
column10: Writes vertex_groups for blend weight — shader-pipeline aware.
column11: Flat plane with trivial Gaussian bumps. Blend is linear across x. No actual biome-specific geometry (roots, moss hummocks, puddles).
column12: Height should sample from biome-specific heightmap; blend factor should be noise-displaced not straight-linear.

column9: UE5 Nanite cave kit / Skyrim cave entrance module
column10: Builds a real tunnel ring-loft with natural jitter; correct topology for quad faces.
column11: Symmetric semicircular arch, uniform extrusion — no irregular mouth shape, no stalactites/lintel erosion, no interior branching.
column12: Randomize arch shape (non-semicircle), vary ring profile along depth, add debris apron + rock framing.

column9: Megascans cliff scans / Quixel rock kit / Gaea Rock node
column10: Deterministic grid with per-vertex Gaussian displacement; returns clean MeshSpec.
column11: Single Gaussian-noise pass with sinusoidal mask — no fracture planes, no strata layering, no overhangs. AAA cliffs use scanned heightfields or rule-based joint systems.
column12: Add stacked strata noise, fault-plane displacement, overhang support (Y can exceed 0.3*sin curve), and weathered talus at base.

column9: Horizon Forbidden West waterfall / RDR2 volumetric cascade
column10: Produces stepped cascade + pool disk — 3D volume (per user's 'never flat plane' rule for falls).
column11: Curtain is a flat-ish two-row strip (checks feedback_waterfall_must_have_volume — this IS almost flat). Pool is a single-segment fan disk. No mist zone, no foam edge, no thickness taper — exactly the failure mode called out in memory.
column12: Curtain needs front curvature >=3 segments, thickness taper top->bottom, plunge pool needs bowl not flat disc. Integrate with terrain_waterfalls_volumetric profile.

column9: Gaea Erosion2 / World Machine Erosion
column10: Single-region erosion preserving source domain (no normalize/denormalize round-trip), returns flow metadata.
column11: Default 1000 hydraulic iterations is far below AAA (Gaea 10k+ for visible gully detail). Thermal only if explicitly asked.
column12: Raise default to 10k+; auto-scale with resolution.

column9: Gaea canvas sampler at world window
column10: Deterministic world-window sampling with world origin + cell size — tile-safe by default. normalize=False preserves the contract.
column11: Thin wrapper around generate_heightmap; assumes single-scale noise; no macro/meso/micro composition.
column12: Compose tectonic plates + regional distortion + local detail as separate noise layers (Gaea Mountain+Hills+Rocks).

column9: Gaea Erosion2 / Houdini Erosion node
column10: Layered approach: analytical erosion (chunk-parallel deterministic) -> hydraulic droplet -> thermal talus. Respects protected zones per-cell, region scoping with pre-snapshot restore. Per-profile (temperate/arid/alpine) config. Derives deterministic seed.
column11: Default iterations 200-600 are low vs Gaea's 10k+; no explicit sediment transport conservation check; thermal uses only 6 iterations always.
column12: Expose iteration scaling with tile resolution; add mass-balance validation.

column9: Gaea Mountain/Hills base layer
column10: Verifies height channel and records metrics. Idempotent.
column11: Docstring admits it's effectively a no-op — height is populated at state construction. A real macro pass should orchestrate tectonic base + regional distortion. Currently it's a validator masquerading as a generator.
column12: Actually call generate_world_heightmap from authoring intent here, so macro_world owns the base terrain, not state construction.

column9: Gaea Analysis nodes (Slope/Convexity/Flow/Ridge)
column10: Delegates to terrain_masks.compute_base_masks which produces slope/curvature/concavity/convexity/ridge/basin/saliency — all the analysis channels a AAA pipeline expects.
column11: supports_region_scope=False (always full-tile) which is correct but expensive; saliency_macro is a single scalar metric name rather than documented formula.
column12: Doc each channel's formula and add tests that compare against a Houdini reference.

column9: Houdini Asserts / UE validation framework
column10: Finite-check on height and NaN/inf scan across channels — solid minimal invariants.
column11: Truly minimal — no range checks (height bounds), no monotone drainage validation, no seam validation with neighbors.
column12: Add range + seam checks so 'validation' earns the name.

column9: Hydraulic geometry (Leopold-Maddock relation)
column10: Correct sqrt scaling — width ~ sqrt(discharge) is the accepted hydraulic-geometry relation. Min/max clamp.
column11: Scale factor is hardcoded default; no regional-calibration hook.
column12: Expose scale_factor via biome/climate params.

column9: Priority-flood spill-point analysis (Gaea Lake node)
column10: Pit detection + flood-fill to spill height + area/inflow records; correct set-based visited tracking.
column11: Naive pit detection (cell < all 8 neighbors) misses plateau lakes where multiple cells share min; spill_z is lowest neighbor, not true watershed spill.
column12: Use priority-flood algorithm (Barnes 2014) for real watershed spill computation.

column9: Heuristic waterfall finder
column10: Sliding-window scan along a river path, finds best drop in bounded horizontal distance, skips past detected falls.
column11: Only scans explicit river_path — won't find falls off the given path; best_drop is scalar, not rate (drop/horiz).
column12: Score by drop rate (drop/horiz_dist); also run independently on drainage raster.

column9: Houdini flow-following / Gaea Flow Trace
column10: D8 descent with visited-set, pit detection, accumulation-drop guard against infinite loops.
column11: Uses Python list for path — okay for single rivers; min_accumulation check '> 0' allows starting cell below threshold.
column12: Vectorize for bulk source processing.

column9: UE5 volumetric fog / Horizon Forbidden West localized fog sheets
column10: Per-biome rules, area-based count calculation using volume footprint per shape, deterministic.
column11: Uniform random placement — no clustering around terrain features (god_rays should emit from tree gaps, fireflies near water). No occlusion/collision awareness. Volume area math for cone uses (h*0.5)^2 — arbitrary approximation.
column12: Accept heightmap + masks (ridge, water, canopy) and bias placement by feature affinity per volume type.

column9: UE5 volumetric proxy mesh
column10: Three shape branches (box, 12-vertex icosphere-like, 8-segment cone). Deterministic.
column11: Icosphere is actually a dodecahedron arrangement (uses 12 verts but faces have errors — 'next_next if next_next <= segments else 1' in cone logic is suspicious and produces degenerate tris). Mesh resolution is too coarse for any close-up.
column12: Replace with proper icosphere subdivision (subdiv=1 gives 42 verts); fix cone face generation math.

column9: UE5 GPU stat scope estimator
column10: Simple linear cost model + threshold-based recommendation. Easy to tune.
column11: Uses arbitrary thresholds (15/30/60/100) with no ms-budget grounding; assumes all particle/distortion volumes cost the same.
column12: Calibrate against actual GPU profile data from a reference scene.

column9: Gaea Erosion applied at coast
column10: Recomputes wave energy on demand; respects rock_hardness for differential erosion (softer erodes more).
column11: hints_wave_dir=0.0 hardcoded — should pull from state hints; max 3m drop per pass is fixed.
column12: Thread wave dir through and parameterize max drop.

column9: Sea of Thieves wave-energy modeling / Houdini coastal exposure analysis
column10: Real physical model: shoreline band (gaussian around sea level), directional exposure from gradient dot-product with wave direction, clipped properly. Vectorized numpy.
column11: Hardcoded 5m decay constant for shoreline band; no fetch-length modeling.
column12: Parameterize decay; add fetch (distance from incoming wave source).

column9: UE5 water tidal mask
column10: Clean vectorized band + linear taper outside; writes to stack.tidal; accepts zero-range gracefully.
column11: Linear taper; real intertidal zones have exponential drainage patterns.
column12: Exponential taper optional; integrate with wet_rock merge.

column9: SpeedTree coastal kit / World Creator coast node
column10: Four styles (rocky/sandy/cliffs/harbor) with distinct shore profiles + feature kits + material zones; validates inputs; returns mesh + features + material_zones + shoreline_profile.
column11: Uses hash-based pseudo-noise (_hash_noise) which is low-quality compared to OpenSimplex; style-specific elevation logic is hardcoded if/elif chain; cliff z-step is a discrete jump ('if land_factor > 0.3: z = base_elev * min(1,...)').
column12: Swap hash_noise for proper 2D OpenSimplex; smooth cliff z-profile; parameterize per-style in a config dict.

column10: Wires hints (sea_level, tidal_range, wave_dir, apply_retreat) from state.intent.composition_hints — composable. Reports wave energy metrics.

column10: Extracts heightmap from mesh, uses _detect_grid_dims (non-square safe), WorldHeightTransform for signed-range preservation (no corruption of negative-elevation worlds), waypointed multi-segment carve, derives river surface Z separately from bed Z, smooths both, returns path_points and bed_points in world coords.

column9: Gaea Layer / World Machine Layer View / Houdini Heightfield Layer
column10: Enforces a validated blend-mode enum, uses __slots__, zeroed float64 numpy storage, and clamps strength to [0,1] for predictable blend behavior.

column9: Layer deserialization
column10: Mirrors to_dict exactly, uses defaults for missing blend_mode/strength, and reshapes to declared shape.
column11: No schema versioning, no validation of data length vs shape, crashes if 'data' key missing — not robust for forward/backward compatibility.
column12: Add a 'version' field, validate len(data) == shape[0]*shape[1], and support npy/base64 payloads.

column9: Layer serialization in Gaea .tor / UE Landscape layer cache
column10: Simple round-trippable dict matching from_dict with full data included.
column11: Serializes the entire heightmap as a plain Python list via tolist() — for a 512x512 layer that is 256K JSON-encoded floats stored on a Blender ID-property; no compression, no npz fallback, massive file bloat.
column12: Use base64-encoded numpy .npy bytes (or numpy.save into a BytesIO) with a schema version, and store large blobs as external .npz files referenced by hash.

column9: Gaea brush ops / UE Landscape sculpt brushes (raise/lower/smooth/noise)
column10: Correctly maps world→grid via terrain_origin center-offset, supports 5 operations, uses elliptical bounds and smooth falloff, and returns affected cell count.
column11: Pure-Python double loop over the brush rect kills performance on 1024+ grids; 'smooth' uses a 3x3 box (not Gaussian); 'noise' is per-cell gauss (unbounded, non-spatially-coherent — AAA uses simplex/perlin at brush scale); 'stamp' replaces instead of blends.
column12: Vectorize via numpy masked ops, replace smooth with scipy gaussian_filter or separable 5x5 Gaussian, replace noise with simplex noise sampled at brush-scale frequency, and change stamp to add/blend with existing heights.

column9: UE Landscape import height stamp / Gaea Stamp apply
column10: Correct world→grid mapping, elliptical bounds, and per-cell falloff gating.
column11: The blend math `edge_falloff*(1-falloff) + edge_falloff*falloff` algebraically collapses to just edge_falloff — the falloff parameter is inert; nearest-integer stamp sampling (no bilinear); additive only (no min/max/replace modes); rotation is unsupported.
column12: Fix the degenerate blend formula (lerp between hard and soft edges properly), add bilinear stamp sampling, expose add/max/replace/multiply blend modes, and add a rotation parameter.

column9: Houdini Heightfield Erode Thermal / Gaea Thermal node
column10: Correct 4-neighbor talus-based slump with excess-fraction redistribution, stable iteration loop, and sensible defaults.
column11: Pure-Python double loop is 100x slower than a numpy vectorized version; uses 4-neighborhood only (no diagonals) so scree fans look axis-aligned; no angle-of-repose per-material layer; returns .tolist() blowing memory for 512+ maps.
column12: Vectorize with numpy shift operations (np.roll + max), add 8-neighborhood with sqrt(2) distance scaling, and expose per-cell talus from a mask for multi-material slopes.

column9: Gaea Erosion brush / World Machine Erosion / Houdini Heightfield Erode
column10: Three named modes under a single brush API, preserves source value range post-NaN scrub, and uses falloff-weighted transfer.
column11: Hydraulic model has no sediment/water channels — it is a slope-redistribution stencil with a hardcoded 0.1 factor, nothing like the Mei-Hill or particle-based hydraulic solvers used by Gaea/Houdini; wind erosion is literally gauss() with fixed +X deposit; thermal duplicates apply_thermal_erosion weakly; pure-Python nested loops make it unusable for brushes over ~64px.
column12: Replace hydraulic with a particle-based droplet solver (Hans-Beyer) or MEI full-shallow-water with sediment/water/flux fields; vectorize with numpy; add anisotropic wind with a direction vector.

column9: Gaea/World Machine brush falloff curve / UE Landscape brush falloff presets
column10: Provides 4 named falloff shapes (smooth cosine, sharp quadratic, linear, constant) with clear clamping and a sensible error on unknown type.
column11: No custom curve/ramp support — AAA brush tools expose user-authored Bezier/AnimationCurve falloffs and additional presets (smoothstep, sphere, inverse-square).
column12: Accept an optional list-of-(t,value) control-point curve or smoothstep variant, and cache the four built-in shapes as numpy LUTs for vectorized callers.

column9: Houdini Heightfield Flow / Gaea Flow node
column10: Correct textbook D8 flow direction + top-down flow-accumulation by sorted height + full drainage-basin labelling with cycle guard.
column11: Pure-Python triple-nested loop makes it prohibitive for >256 grids (minutes on 512); D8 has known artifacts (parallel channel stripes) — AAA uses D-infinity (Tarboton) or multiple-flow-direction; no pit-filling pre-pass so internal pits create spurious basins; returns .tolist() which balloons memory.
column12: Vectorize flow direction with numpy roll tricks, add a Planchon-Darboux pit-fill pre-pass, offer D-inf / MFD alternatives, and return numpy arrays optionally.

column9: Gaea Route / Houdini Heightfield Project Path / UE Landscape Spline Deform
column10: Implements four deformation modes (carve/raise/flatten/smooth) with a tidy core+blend-zone falloff split and proper flatten-to-spline-height interpolation.
column11: Only modifies vertex Z without blending lateral shoulders into valley walls; 'smooth' mode degenerates to a weak pull-toward-spline-Z with no neighborhood averaging; no cross-section profile (flat bottom, bank angles, cutbanks).
column12: Add a user-provided cross-section curve (width vs depth) and a proper Laplacian smoothing pass for mode='smooth'; raymarch along spline normal to produce true trench/berm profiles.

column9: Gaea Stamp / Houdini Heightfield Noise/Stamp library
column10: Six named radial stamps (crater/mesa/hill/valley/plateau/ridge) plus custom heightmap passthrough with a clean enum-validated API.
column11: All built-in stamps are trivially analytic radial functions — a real crater has an asymmetric rim with ejecta rays and a central peak; a mesa has fracture patterns; these look like math primitives, not terrain features; fixed 64px resolution truncates detail.
column12: Replace built-ins with real heightmap tiles (PNG/EXR library) — craters with ejecta blankets, mesas with ridge noise overlay, plus FFT-resampling to arbitrary resolution; keep analytic shapes only as fallbacks.

column9: Houdini xyzdist() / UE Landscape Spline closest-point query
column10: Correct XY-projected segment closest-point with normalized t-along-spline output and proper degenerate-segment handling.
column11: Brute-force O(N*M) scan of every polyline segment per vertex — AAA toolchains use spatial acceleration (BVH or uniform grid) and typically query in 3D not just XY.
column12: Build a segment AABB grid or BVH once per spline and query per-vertex; optionally offer 3D distance for vertical splines.

column9: Houdini Spline SOP / UE5 Spline Component evaluation
column10: Clean Catmull-Rom-style tangent estimation via cubic Bezier segments with configurable tension produces C1-smooth polylines suitable for road/river paths.
column11: Uses fixed uniform parameter sampling (no arc-length reparameterization), so point density clumps on tight curves and spreads on straights — AAA spline tooling samples by arc length for even corridor width.
column12: Add arc-length reparameterization pass (cumulative segment length lookup) and expose a tolerance-based adaptive subdivision for curvature.

column9: Gaea Combine node / Houdini Heightfield Layer merge
column10: Proper ordered blend pipeline with ADD/SUBTRACT/MAX/MIN/MULTIPLY modes, strength scaling, and nearest-neighbor resize when layer shapes differ from base.
column11: Nearest-neighbor resize produces blocky aliasing on mismatched layers; MULTIPLY is hardcoded as (1+lh) which is idiosyncratic; no OVERLAY/SCREEN/SOFT-LIGHT modes; no per-layer mask channel.
column12: Use bilinear/lanczos resize, add overlay/screen/soft-light, honor a per-layer mask, and expose an explicit multiply semantic (direct multiply vs 1+offset).

column9: Batch flatten across multiple building footprints
column10: Straightforward sequential application of flatten_terrain_zone with per-zone parameters and safe key defaults.
column11: Sequential application means later zones fight earlier ones in overlap regions (last-writer-wins) — AAA batch flatten resolves overlaps via max/min/priority or unified mask; no overlap warning; O(Z*H*W) with Z zones, no spatial bucketing.
column12: Build a combined mask with per-cell priority/blend and apply in one pass, or expose an overlap-resolution mode (max/min/weighted).

column9: UE Landscape Flatten brush / Gaea Terrace flat zone
column10: Vectorized numpy implementation with smoothstep C1 blend, auto target height from in-radius mean, and careful source-range preservation (no silent [0,1] clip) with NaN/inf scrubbing.
column11: Circular only (name says 'zone' but no ellipse or rectangle support); blend is isotropic and cannot match building footprint shapes; seed parameter is unused (documented 'reserved').
column12: Accept (radius_x, radius_y) or a polygon mask; add optional noise-perturbed blend edge using seed; support rectangular/OBB footprints aligned to building rotation.

column9: UE Landscape Erosion sculpt brush
column10: Cleanly wires bmesh read/write around compute_erosion_brush with _detect_grid_dims-based reshape and parameter validation.
column11: Inherits all weaknesses of compute_erosion_brush (toy hydraulic/wind); no undo; blocks Blender main thread during slow pure-Python erosion; doesn't rebuild normals after displacement so shading looks wrong.
column12: Move compute into a numpy/numba path, call mesh.calc_normals_split or bmesh normal update post-mutation, and add a progress/cancel mechanism.

column9: UE Foliage/Blueprint SnapToGround / Unity Terrain.SampleHeight
column10: Proper world↔local matrix handling for ray_cast, optional normal alignment using axis-angle rotation, per-object result reporting, and configurable offset.
column11: Single downward ray with no fallback (misses on overhangs/cliffs); normal alignment replaces the entire rotation_euler, destroying any authored yaw; no multi-sample averaging for stable placement on noisy terrain; no parallelization for large object lists.
column12: Preserve object yaw by only tilting to normal around the vertical; add multi-sample footprint averaging; offer a fallback raycast from object centroid and an up-ray option for ceiling snap.

column9: UE Landscape Spline tool / Unity Terrain path carve
column10: Correctly wraps the pure-logic function with bmesh round-trip, validates object existence and point count, and clamps samples_per_segment.
column11: Operates on object-local coords but the pure-logic uses world-space spline points — no matrix_world transform, so splines break when the terrain is moved/scaled/rotated; no undo support or mesh-data integrity checks.
column12: Transform spline_points through obj.matrix_world.inverted() before calling compute_spline_deformation, and register with bpy.ops.ed.undo_push for editor undo.

column9: UE Landscape Flatten tool on an arbitrary mesh
column10: Handles arbitrary non-grid meshes by binning vertices into a grid, fills NaN cells via column/global mean, and applies per-vertex delta to preserve mesh topology rather than reshape-assuming row-major.
column11: Grid binning loses sub-cell detail and reintroduces aliasing; fetches delta via grid[row_idx, col_idx] nearest-neighbor (no bilinear); target_height normalization recomputes z_min/z_max on the binned grid not the vertices; two dead `_ = max(..., 1e-6)` lines signal unfinished normalization logic; ellipse radius_x/radius_y collapses to a single averaged flat_radius so non-circular zones aren't actually supported despite the param surface.
column12: Use bilinear interpolation when sampling delta back to vertices, honor radius_x vs radius_y as a true ellipse in the pure-logic call, remove dead code, and compute target normalization from the real vertex Z range.

column9: UE Landscape layer UI / Gaea layer panel
column10: Full CRUD routing (add/remove/modify/flatten/list) with JSON-serialized state persisted on the mesh custom property and layer-name lookup.
column11: Every mutation re-serializes the full layer stack to a JSON string on an ID-property — will bloat .blend files and fail silently at ID-property size limits; flatten_layers assumes vertex order equals row-major reshape which breaks on non-grid meshes despite _detect_grid_dims; no undo integration.
column12: Persist layers as an external .npz per terrain keyed by hash, chunk large layers, and add depsgraph-aware undo.

column9: UE Landscape Stamp tool
column10: Clean bmesh integration, parameter defaults, and reuse of pure-logic compute_stamp_heightmap + apply_stamp_to_heightmap.
column11: Inherits the inert-falloff bug and primitive stamp shapes from its dependencies; hardcoded 64x64 stamp resolution; no rotation; uses object-local terrain_size from obj.dimensions which breaks when mesh is transformed.
column12: Plumb resolution/rotation/blend-mode params, transform position through inverse matrix_world, and fix the falloff degenerate blend.

column9: Skyrim/ESO cave portal kit
column10: Per-archetype framing count (2 or 3), computes lintel position from entrance_height, vegetation_screen predicate, occlusion_shelf intent.
column11: Framing rocks are just world positions + radius — no actual shape variation, no per-archetype material hint.
column12: Specify rock asset kit per archetype + size variance + biased noise for position.

column9: Voxel carve along spline (UE5 Voxel Plugin)
column10: Proper vectorized np.mgrid footprint per waypoint; taper by distance from path center; deepest-delta-per-cell merge; populates cave_candidate bool mask.
column11: Flat 2D footprint — heightmap can't represent true 3D caves (the delta only lowers terrain surface). For real caves this needs voxel/mesh-boolean backend.
column12: Wire through to a voxel field (terrain_advanced) so caves are true 3D volumes.

column9: Houdini curve-based cave authoring / UE5 Voxel Farm cave curve
column10: Per-archetype path shape (LAVA_TUBE straight, FISSURE vertical, KARST plunge-then-arm, GLACIAL meander, SEA_GROTTO shallow). Deterministic rng.
column11: All paths are 2D polylines with simple trigonometric jitter — no branching, no chamber nodes, no inclination variation within archetype.
column12: Add branching (Y-splits), chamber hubs along path, per-archetype branch probability.

column9: UE5 wetness mask shader driver
column10: Vectorized np.mgrid per waypoint with proper max-merge; preserves existing wet_rock.
column11: Linear falloff — real damp patterns follow seepage paths (downslope preference).
column12: Bias falloff downslope of path.

column9: MCP handler shim over cave authoring
column10: Bridges params dict to pass_caves via synthetic state; returns debris/frame geometry intent.
column11: Uses synthetic state builder — real scene_read may not be present; chamber mesh is a simple box loft.
column12: Prefer actual scene state over synthetic; richer chamber mesh.

column9: UE PCG cave authoring graph
column10: End-to-end: scene_read-derived entrances, per-cave deterministic seed via hash-mix, protected-zone cell check, archetype pick, path+carve+frame+debris+damp, cave_candidate protected masking, side_effects recorded for downstream bundles, validation.
column11: Only processes scene-read supplied entrances (no auto-discovery from heightmap features); cell_count recomputed per loop.
column12: Add auto-entrance detection using cliff-edge + slope + basin proximity when scene_read is empty.

column9: UE5 PCG debris scatter
column10: Deterministic, density-scaled, normal-jitter around path; caps count 200 to bound work.
column11: No clustering (debris typically piles), no collision with entrance frame; all debris same type.
column12: Add Poisson clustering + per-cluster type.

column9: Houdini Heightfield carve + side tributary erosion (e.g. RDR2 Grand Canyon, Uncharted Lost Legacy canyons)
column10: Produces a coherent three-piece mesh (floor + two walls) with wall-height-based material banding (wet_rock low, canyon_wall mid) and floor path waypoints useful for nav.
column11: Canyon is a straight rectilinear trench along X with only mild y-offset wall noise; no meander, no talus slopes, no stratified horizontal rock layers, and 'side caves' are only metadata dicts with zero actual mesh carved into the walls.
column12: Build canyon centerline via meandering spline, carve walls from a base heightmap with multi-octave stratified erosion, generate real boolean cave voids at each side cave, and add scree/debris cones at wall bases.

column9: Uncharted / Tomb Raider climbable cliff with ledge kit, or UE Landscape Layer Blend with overhang tool.
column10: Two-octave noise on face, explicit overhang lean at the top quartile with underside sheet, and a separate ledge-path ribbon usable for climbing.
column11: Base cliff is still a single planar heightfield tilted at the top (not true overhangs with undercut), cave entrances are metadata only with no holes punched in the face, and ledge ribbon is a flat two-vertex strip with no thickness or railings/handholds.
column12: Use a displaced volumetric slab (front + top + underside stitched) so overhang becomes a real shelf, boolean-carve cave entrances through the face, and extrude the ledge into a 3D walkable shelf with chamfered edge.

column9: Avatar/Horizon floating islands or Zelda TOTK sky islands; Houdini Voronoi-fractured rock clusters.
column10: Each rock is a real latitude/longitude tessellated sphere with two-octave noise on both radius and z, proper top/mid/bottom cap handling and even a fallback for mismatched segment counts; chain link geometry is actual extruded 4-sided rings, not just metadata.
column11: Chain 'links' are open cylindrical shafts stacked vertically (not interlocked torus rings), rocks are fundamentally noisy spheres with no sharp fractured planes or crystalline protrusions, undersides look identical to tops (same material differentiation is purely tag-based), and there is no island flat top / grass zone typical of fantasy float rocks.
column12: Replace sphere base with Voronoi-fractured polyhedra giving flat facets, differentiate top via flattened cap + grass mask, make chain links actual torus primitives interlocking at 90-degree alternation, and add crystal vein mesh geometry rather than just material tag.

column9: Yellowstone-style travertine terrace (RDR2 Cotorra Springs, Horizon hot springs) with layered sinter steps.
column10: Three-tier terraced ring structure with per-tier z offset and radial noise, plus a cone vent with base/mid/tip rings and sulfur-crust material, which is more structured than a flat decal.
column11: Pool is a fan-triangulated flat disc (no concavity toward center despite the comment), terraces are only 3 rigid concentric rings of equal width (not scalloped basins with pour-lips), and there is no water surface mesh, no steam vent placement, and the vent cone is just 3 rings.
column12: Model terraces as stacked basin polygons with irregular pour-lips carved per tier, add a real water surface inside each basin, generate steam/sulfur plume emitter points, and vary terrace counts by pool_radius.

column9: God of War Ragnarok ice caves / Horizon FW Cauldrons with volumetric ice, parallax refraction, and icicle clusters.
column10: Per-stalactite cone with variable length/radius, noise-displaced ring geometry and tip triangles; ice wall is a genuine 2D grid with FBM undulation and a curvature-based refraction material split.
column11: Stalactite material banding uses `kt` variable that is actually the OUTER loop counter `k` from a previous loop (variable captured incorrectly — bug: `kt` in the mat_indices block references the last stalactite's last ring k/cone_rings, not current face position), 'refraction zones' are randomly sampled metadata disconnected from the actual curvature classification applied to faces, ice wall is a flat displaced plane (no thickness), and there is no floor/stalagmite, no pool, no volumetric ice.
column12: Fix the kt-scoping bug so banding tracks current ring properly; extrude the ice wall into a volume with frosted interior; add matching stalagmites and frozen pool mesh; derive refraction_zones from the actual high-curvature face centers rather than random sampling.

column9: Horizon Burning Shores lava channels or Kilauea reference with pahoehoe crust folds and incandescent fissures.
column10: Sinusoidal centerline with noise-modulated perturbation, correct per-segment tangent/perpendicular frame for the cross-section sweep, five-band cross-section profile (raised solid rock, raised crust, depressed lava) with height variation, and heat_zones metadata parameterized by flow position.
column11: Flow is still a single-strip ribbon — no branching, no pillow/tube morphology, no solidification fingers; crust edges are symmetric with simple linear height profile, no a'a vs pahoehoe variation; lava surface is the same continuous grid as the rock banks with no lip or embankment discontinuity; heat zones are uniformly distributed along t with no flow-physics grounding.
column12: Replace ribbon with a 2D flow-accumulation field producing branching channels, model crust as a separate floating shell above a lower hot-lava surface (two meshes) with broken crust patches, and derive heat_zones from channel-width narrowings/drop points instead of uniform sampling.

column9: Real natural arch (Delicate Arch / Mesa Arch) as sculpted erosion of sandstone block, or Houdini Voronoi-fracture + erosion.
column10: Sweep uses a correctly oriented Frenet-like frame (tangent in XZ, normal perpendicular) and ring cross-section allows noise displacement, giving a non-trivial closed tube.
column11: The arch is a swept elliptical tube (torus-arc) with single-sample noise per ring segment — that is the archetypal 'stretched torus' approach called out by the rubric; real natural arches are eroded rock masses with flat tops, scalloped undersides, and asymmetric pillar bases, none of which are modeled. Pillars are simple tapered 4-vert boxes.
column12: Start from a solid rock mass (blockout box), boolean-subtract an elliptical tunnel, then apply per-layer strata displacement and wind-erosion noise on the underside so the shape reads as eroded sandstone, not a bent pipe.

column9: Tomb Raider/Uncharted cenote or Sima de las Cotorras with vertical walls, vegetation shelves, and water pool bottom.
column10: Multi-ring construction (outer rim, inner rim, stacked wall rings, floor fan) with FBM wall roughness, per-depth material banding, and rubble pieces as real 8-vert displaced box meshes.
column11: Walls are still rotationally symmetric (only 15% narrowing at bottom, no undercuts or bell shape), rubble boxes are axis-aligned and identical topology, the bottom cave is metadata-only with no actual opening cut into the wall, and there is no water/vegetation shelf despite sinkholes typically having both.
column12: Allow non-axisymmetric radius profile (bell or hourglass), boolean-carve the bottom cave into the wall mesh, scatter rocks with varied topology and rotation, and add a mid-depth vegetation shelf ring.

column9: Far Cry/Witcher 3 bog biome with hummock stamps, standing water masks, and island dry-zone classification.
column10: Real FBM base, O(N^2) radial hummock/island stamping with quadratic and quartic falloffs, BFS flood fill to extract connected water-zone bounding boxes, and water-coverage metric for downstream asset scattering.
column11: Resolution scales as size/2 (so a 50m swamp is only 25x25 = 625 quads = ~2m quads) which is too coarse for AAA; no channel/river carving, no peat/hammock micro-morphology, and water is tagged via material only — there is no actual water plane mesh.
column12: Raise resolution to sub-meter, carve drainage channels with hydraulic erosion pass, emit a separate water surface mesh aligned to water_level, and output root/reed scatter masks instead of just material IDs.

column9: Horizon Forbidden West / Genshin waterfall with volumetric water plume, pool caustics, and mist VFX; Gaea water-carving layer.
column10: Has direction-aware rotation for arbitrary facing, emits pool/cave/splash metadata for downstream VFX, and tags wet/dry material zones along the cliff.
column11: Ledges are literally single quads (no side/bottom faces), pool is a crude 16-sided fan triangle disc with no lip or undercut, 'cave behind' is pure metadata with no geometry carved, and there is no water mesh, no plunge basin curvature, and no erosion behind/below the falls.
column12: Generate real boxed ledges with front undercuts, carve a real cave void behind the flow with SDF boolean, build a volumetric water plume mesh (tapered prism, not plane) and a contoured plunge pool with distinct lip, and output mist emitter positions.

column9: Corrupted-zone overlay shader
column10: Straightforward RGBA blend toward corruption purple; pushes alpha to special channel.
column11: Uniform tint — no per-vertex corruption variance; hardcoded purple (0.12, 0.04, 0.14) from 'linear sRGB' comment.
column12: Sample corruption_map per-vertex for spatial variance.

column9: UE Landscape layer slope/height auto-assign
column10: Four-zone classification (ground/slopes/cliffs/water_edges) per-face via normal + height + water_level; distributes across palette materials by face index for variance.
column11: Modulo face index distribution is deterministic but visually linear — no spatial coherence (adjacent faces can flip material).
column12: Use spatial hash (face center) with blue noise for variance.

column9: UE5 Vertex-color splatmap (4-channel)
column10: Averages per-vertex zone weights across adjacent faces; normalizes RGBA to sum 1; isolated-vertex fallback.
column11: Static zone->channel weights ignore biome-specific preferences (a swamp shouldn't give .6 grass + 0 rock uniformly); no smoothing across vertices.
column12: Pull zone weights per-biome from palette; Laplacian smooth.

column9: Witcher 3 / ESO biome-boundary splat blend
column10: Runs auto_assign_terrain_layers for both biomes then smoothsteps between them with noise-displaced boundary — clean organic edge.
column11: Axis-aligned boundary only (x or y); _simple_noise_2d is a hash-based approximation, not true Simplex; amplitude is in world units but scale is normalized.
column12: Support arbitrary polyline boundary + OpenSimplex noise.

column9: UE5 Landscape auto-material RGBA (grass/dirt/rock/special)
column10: Fully vectorized numpy (previously Python double loop — now <200ms on 512² per docstring), height+slope+moisture fusion, special-band policy with ground-restriction, moisture tint, final normalization preserving alpha. This is production quality.
column11: Linear slope thresholds; no anisotropic lighting hint; moisture tint is limited (>0.7 or <0.3).
column12: Soften slope transition with smoothstep; expose moisture curve.

column9: Houdini node cache / UE5 asset version checkpointing
column10: Captures Unity-export metadata (world_bounds, height range, cell_size, coord system) so checkpoints are round-trippable. Parent linkage for undo chain.
column11: NPZ files pile up over long sessions — no rotation/pruning.
column12: Add checkpoint retention policy.

column9: UE Landscape layer exclusion / Horizon zero region lock
column10: Correctly distinguishes full-cover vs partial intersection; lets per-cell masks do finer policy.
column11: Region BBox is axis-aligned only — polygon zones not supported.
column12: Accept polygon zones for hero-art protection (tournament-quality Skyrim-like zone masks).

column9: Houdini TOP pass executor / UE5 PCG graph node runner
column10: Enforces scene-read, protected zones, input-channel prereqs, post-run produces_channels verification, quality-gate with hard/soft severity, visual_validator, content-hash before/after, checkpoint on success. Very thorough contract enforcement.
column11: Failure in the pass records duration but the state may be partly mutated — no transactional rollback of mask_stack. Pragma: no cover on the except clause hides untested error paths.
column12: Snapshot mask_stack before func() and restore on exception (use checkpoint dir already present).

column9: Houdini TOP scheduler
column10: Default sequence is sensible (macro->masks->erosion->validate). Stops on first failure.
column11: No parallel tile dispatch; no partial rollback to last-good checkpoint on failure.
column12: Add multiprocessing per-tile and auto-rollback option.

column9: Houdini HDA deterministic seed chaining / UE5 PCG seed domain
column10: SHA-256 of JSON-encoded tuple, PYTHONHASHSEED-independent, 32-bit masked for numpy RNG. Production-correct.
column11: JSON encoding adds overhead vs a direct bytes hash, but negligible.
column12: Cache on intent to avoid re-hashing per-pass in tight loops if needed.

column9: UE PCG node registration
column10: Declarative definitions with requires/produces channels — enables static contract verification.
column11: Hard-coded 4 passes; no way to compose profiles.
column12: Load profile from YAML to support game-specific pass graphs.

column9: UE Landscape brush footprint / Houdini heightfield mask by radius
column10: Correctly uses squared-distance culling in XY before taking the sqrt, returning a sparse weight list.
column11: O(N) linear scan over every vertex with no spatial acceleration (KD-tree/grid/BVH), XY-only so projects wrong on sloped terrain, no pressure/tilt/azimuth, no anisotropic (elliptical) brush, no alpha-texture mask, and no stroke dab spacing - AAA tools use screen-space projection along the view ray with a spatial index.
column12: Add KD-tree/spatial grid accel, ray-project the brush onto the surface (not XY plane), support pressure curve + elliptical shape + alpha-image mask + stroke spacing, and compute weight over geodesic distance for steep slopes.

column9: ZBrush Flatten/Planar/Trim Dynamic, UE Landscape Flatten (with eye-dropper)
column10: Computes a real brush-local average rather than a fixed global plane.
column11: Flattens to unweighted mean Z of affected verts (not a fitted plane), ignores brush weights in the average (bias toward edge verts), no user-set target height / eye-dropper pick, no plane-from-first-contact 'trim' mode, no angle/slope constraint - AAA flatten brushes plane-fit via least-squares and offer 'flatten to target', 'ramp', 'contrast' modes.
column12: Compute a weighted least-squares plane from the affected verts, expose modes (average / first-hit / user-target / plane-fit), add contrast-blur parameter and ramp/slope constraint.

column9: ZBrush Standard (inverted) / UE Landscape Sculpt (lower)
column10: Symmetric to raise, tiny and correct for its trivial spec.
column11: Just a sign-flip of raise - no normal-aligned direction, no floor/minimum clamp, no distinct 'dig' behavior (AAA dig brushes carve along inverse-normal with a sharper falloff and terrain-floor clamp), and shares all of raise's gaps.
column12: Share a single directional-displacement kernel with raise, expose direction enum (UP/DOWN/NORMAL/INV_NORMAL), add min-height clamp, pressure, and dig-shape option.

column9: ZBrush Standard/Inflate brush, UE Landscape Sculpt (raise)
column10: Simple deterministic weighted displacement, easy to test.
column11: Displaces purely along world Z with no surface-normal option, no accumulation curve / per-dab falloff, no max-height clamp, no layer/undo stack, no pressure-sensitive strength, and no noise/jitter - an AAA raise brush offsets along averaged normal with stroke accumulation and clamps.
column12: Offset along smoothed vertex normal (toggle normal vs Z), add per-stroke accumulation/clamp, pressure curve, optional noise modulation, and feed into a layered height buffer for non-destructive sculpting.

column9: ZBrush Smooth brush, Blender sculpt Smooth (Laplacian/surface/enhance-detail)
column10: Uses real 1-ring adjacency (not a fixed-radius 4-neighbor hack), giving a proper uniform Laplacian on Z.
column11: Only smooths Z (not full 3D position), uniform weighting rather than cotangent/area weights (causes shrinkage on irregular meshes), single Jacobi iteration per call (no iteration count), no Taubin lambda/mu shrinkage compensation, no detail-preserving smooth, no boundary handling - ZBrush/Blender offer Laplacian, Surface, Enhance-Detail, Taubin, and relax modes.
column12: Add iterations param, support cotangent weights, Taubin lambda/mu to prevent shrinkage, a surface-smooth mode that preserves XY and only relaxes along normal, plus relax-topology and enhance-detail variants.

column9: World Machine/Gaea stamp macro, Houdini heightfield stamp, ZBrush Alpha stamp, UE Landscape Erosion/Stamp brush
column10: Performs explicit UV remap of brush footprint into heightmap grid and clamps UV, with a strength multiplier.
column11: Nearest-neighbor sampling only (no bilinear/bicubic) causing blocky artifacts, axis-aligned square footprint inside a circular brush, no rotation / scale / flip, no blend modes (add/max/min/subtract/replace), no height remap curve, no wrap/tile, no masked alpha channel - AAA stamps support rotation, 8+ blend ops, bicubic sampling, and remap curves.
column12: Add bilinear/bicubic sampling, rotation/scale/flip of the stamp, blend modes (add/max/min/replace/multiply/subtract), height remap curve, tile/wrap option, and a separate mask channel; decouple stamp footprint from circular brush weight.

column9: ZBrush/Mudbox brush falloff curve (customizable curve editor)
column10: Provides four named falloff curves including a proper cosine smooth ramp with clamping and unknown-name guard.
column11: Hard-coded set of 4 curves with no user-definable curve editor, no root/inverse-sharp/middle/gaussian options, and the clamp to 1.5 silently allows out-of-range input where AAA tools expose an editable 2D spline.
column12: Replace with a sampled 1D curve LUT driven by a user-editable spline (bezier/catmull) plus common presets (gauss, sharper, root, constant, custom), matching Blender/ZBrush brush curve UX.

column9: UE Landscape Sculpt mode / Blender Sculpt operator / ZBrush stroke dispatch
column10: Provides a single dispatch entry-point covering 5 operations with clean bmesh lifecycle (try/finally free), validation, and a structured return payload including affected vertex count.
column11: Not a true stroke system - a single dab per call with no stroke spacing / path / smoothing-between-dabs, full mesh read/write every call (O(N) each), no undo stack / layer, no symmetry (X/Y/radial), no multires / sculpt-mode integration, no mask/vertex-group support, no normal/position updates beyond Z, no dirty-rect optimization - AAA sculpt dispatchers stream strokes, write only touched verts, push to an undo stack, and support symmetry + mask + layers.
column12: Accept a stroke (list of samples + pressure), build/maintain a vertex KD-tree + dirty-vert set across dabs, add symmetry planes, mask/vertex-group weighting, undo layer stack, and an option to modify XYZ along normal; batch bmesh writes per stroke not per dab.

column9: Gaea River/Channel carve
column10: Pushes shallow trench along outflow waypoints with proper min-delta merging (keeps deepest).
column11: Square-kernel iteration per waypoint (Python double loop) — slow; linear taper rather than parabolic; no meander.
column12: Vectorize with scipy.ndimage.distance_transform_edt across path mask; add meander.

column9: Gaea Pool/Basin carve
column10: Parabolic bowl is correct — center deepest, 0 at rim. Non-destructive delta approach respects pipeline protocol.
column11: Circular footprint; real plunge pools have directional scour (downstream elongation) from jet impact.
column12: Elliptical footprint oriented along outflow_direction_rad; erode lip downstream.

column9: Horizon Forbidden West river-lip detector / Houdini flow-based feature tagging
column10: Real drainage+drop joint threshold, D8 descent, per-cell confidence blending drainage and drop scores, D8-neighbor dedup to collapse lip rows.
column11: Python nested for-loop — slow on >1k grids; confidence is linear blend (could use logistic).
column12: Vectorize with numpy argmax over D8 stack and np.where thresholds.

column9: UE5 water foam mask
column10: Scales foam by chain.foam_intensity (which is tied to total_drop) — physically motivated.
column11: Same radial profile as mist; no foam along plunge path itself, only pool.
column12: Add foam along full plunge_path and outflow with turbulence-derived intensity.

column9: Horizon Forbidden West localized volumetric fog
column10: Radial falloff from pool center, returns float32 for shader consumption.
column11: Perfect circle — wind direction not modeled; Python inner loops.
column12: Bias mist downwind from chain's outflow vector; vectorize.

column9: Gaea hydrology graph node / RDR2 river pass
column10: Full chain: lips -> solve -> carve pools -> outflow -> foam/mist masks -> wet_rock integration with water_network. Applies pool_delta to height (real mutation). Region scoping. Fixes pipeline-breaks #1, #2, #5 per comments. Issues validated and downgraded to warning.
column11: Caps at 16 chains (soft limit); wet_rock merging uses max instead of accumulate.
column12: Parameterize cap; consider alpha-blend wet_rock accumulation.

column9: Houdini cascade solver / RDR2 waterfall chain authoring
column10: Traces plunge path with plateau detection, records multi-tier drop segments, derives pool radius from sqrt(total_drop), computes outflow direction, generates deterministic chain_id from world position.
column11: Plateau detection is heuristic (plateau_hits>=2); outflow cap of 32 cells may truncate long cascades; no actual overhang geometry — chain is centerline only.
column12: Add overhang detection at lip (undercut carving), longer outflow cap, multi-drop merging with confluence.

column9: Custom AAA mesh-contract validator
column10: Enforces min vertex density per meter, non-zero thickness, front_curvature >=3 — directly addresses user's 'must be 3D volumetric' rule from memory.
column11: Only checks profile params, not actual mesh generated — relies on downstream to honor profile.
column12: Round-trip check: generate mesh, measure vert density, feed back into validation.
